mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is still elevated while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, markets infer prolonged accommodation and higher future inflation risk. That combination pushes the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days as term premia and expected inflation repricing dominate.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with policy still highly accommodative
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS remain elevated/low in the stated configuration but the 10-year yield falls in more than 60% of 20-day windows, or if the sign of the yield response is negative for two consecutive rolling quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and lower expected policy rates. Over the next 5 trading days, that shock pulls the 10-year Treasury yield lower as growth expectations and inflation expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 pp versus the prior monthly reading
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-triggered episodes are followed by rising 10-year yields in more than 55% of cases, or if the average 5-day yield change after the trigger is nonnegative across any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in INDPRO with stable or falling unemployment indicates stronger real activity and tighter capacity. That combination raises expected future rates and term premia, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum with flat or declining UNRATE does not correspond to higher 20-day yields in at least 60% of instances, or if the average yield change is negative for two back-to-back expansion subperiods.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS contracts from the prior month, retail demand is weakening and nominal growth expectations fall. That soft demand impulse leads the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next month as investors price slower consumption and easier policy.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month RSAFS declines are followed by higher 20-day Treasury yields in more than 55% of triggered observations, or if the conditional mean yield change is not below zero over any 24-month evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in HOUST and GDP growth signals a housing-led demand slowdown that typically feeds lower inflation expectations. Over the next 60 trading days, that weak-demand mix should push the 10-year Treasury yield downward.
failure_condition: Reject if falling HOUST plus decelerating GDPC1 is not associated with lower 60-day yields in at least 60% of episodes, or if the conditional yield move turns positive during two consecutive housing downturn cycles.

mechanism_narrative: If FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, the market treats policy as restrictive and expects inflation to cool, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days as expected future short rates are repriced lower.
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive FEDFUNDS episodes do not produce lower 20-day yields in at least 55% of cases, or if CPIAUCSL fails to decelerate while the yield nonetheless rises persistently after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS count together with a higher UNRATE confirms labor-market deterioration beyond revision noise. That joint deterioration should pull the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 5 trading days as growth expectations are marked down quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up combination fails to precede lower 5-day yields in more than 55% of matched instances, or if the average yield response is positive over a 2-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 after prior weakness signals improving real demand and rising inflation pressure over a quarter-long window. That should raise the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations and term premia reprice upward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 increases versus its prior quarterly reading
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDPC1 rebounds are not associated with higher 60-day yields in at least 60% of episodes, or if CPIAUCSL does not stay firm while the yield response remains negative across two successive quarters.
